name: RAG Chatbot / Knowledge Assistant
description: Retrieval-Augmented Generation chatbot that answers user queries by retrieving relevant documents from a knowledge base, assembling context, and generating responses through a large language model. This is the most common AI pattern in enterprise deployments, combining semantic search with generative AI to provide grounded, domain-specific answers while reducing hallucination risk.
logisticsContext: RAG chatbots are the most deployed AI pattern in logistics, handling rate inquiries, shipment tracking, customs procedures, and internal knowledge access. They process sensitive data including contracted rates, customer PII, and trade compliance information.

nodes.label: User Interface
nodes.description: Chat widget, API endpoint, or Teams/Slack integration where users submit natural language queries. This is the primary attack surface for prompt injection, as all user input enters the system here. Handles rate inquiries, shipment tracking questions, SOP lookups, and customs procedure queries.
vulnerabilities: Direct prompt injection via crafted user input designed to override system instructions
Automated bot abuse sending high-volume queries to exhaust API quotas or model tokens
Session hijacking if conversation state is stored client-side without integrity checks
Cross-site scripting if chat widget renders unsanitized HTML in responses

nodes.label: Input Validation Layer
nodes.description: Sanitizes and validates all incoming user input before it reaches query processing. Enforces character length limits, strips known injection patterns, applies content filtering rules, and rate-limits requests per user session. First line of defense against prompt injection and resource exhaustion.
vulnerabilities: Bypass through encoding tricks (Unicode homoglyphs, base64, URL encoding) that evade pattern matching
Incomplete blocklist allowing novel injection patterns not yet catalogued
Rate limiting circumvention through distributed requests or session rotation
False positives blocking legitimate queries containing technical terminology

nodes.label: Query Processing
nodes.description: Reformulates user queries for optimal retrieval, performs intent classification, expands abbreviations, resolves coreferences from conversation history, and determines which knowledge domains to search. Transforms raw user input into structured retrieval queries.
vulnerabilities: Intent misclassification causing retrieval from wrong knowledge domains or bypassing access controls
Query expansion inadvertently broadening scope to include restricted document collections
Conversation history injection where prior turns contain hidden instructions that influence reformulation

nodes.label: Embedding Model
nodes.description: Converts natural language queries and documents into dense vector representations for semantic similarity search. Typically a transformer model (e.g., text-embedding-ada-002, BGE, E5) that maps text to high-dimensional vectors. Used both at query time and during document ingestion.
vulnerabilities: Supply chain compromise if embedding model weights are sourced from untrusted repositories
Adversarial inputs crafted to produce misleading vector representations that retrieve wrong documents
Model version drift causing inconsistency between query embeddings and previously indexed document embeddings
Embedding inversion attacks that reconstruct sensitive source text from stored vectors

nodes.label: Vector Database
nodes.description: Stores and indexes document embedding vectors for fast approximate nearest-neighbor search. Contains vectorized representations of all knowledge base documents. Performs similarity search to find the most relevant document chunks for a given query vector. Examples: Pinecone, Weaviate, Qdrant, pgvector.
vulnerabilities: Data poisoning through injection of adversarial embeddings that surface malicious content for targeted queries
Unauthorized access to vector indices exposing the semantic content of proprietary documents
Index corruption causing retrieval failures or returning stale document chunks
Metadata filter bypass allowing retrieval of documents outside a user's authorized scope

nodes.label: Knowledge Base
nodes.description: Source document repository containing SOPs, operational manuals, rate cards, customer documentation, customs procedures, trade compliance guides, and internal policies. Documents are chunked, embedded, and indexed into the vector database during ingestion. Data sensitivity varies from public procedures to confidential contracted rates.
vulnerabilities: Indirect prompt injection via malicious content embedded in uploaded documents that executes when retrieved
Data poisoning through insertion of incorrect SOPs or modified rate cards that the LLM presents as authoritative
Sensitive data exposure if confidential rate cards or PII-containing documents lack proper access classification
Stale content causing the chatbot to provide outdated customs procedures or expired rate information
Cross-tenant data leakage if multi-customer documents share the same vector namespace without isolation

nodes.label: Retrieval Pipeline
nodes.description: Orchestrates the retrieval process: performs top-K similarity search against the vector database, applies re-ranking and relevance scoring, filters results by metadata and access controls, and assembles the final context window sent to the LLM. Controls how much and which retrieved content influences the model's response.
vulnerabilities: Context window manipulation where adversarial documents are crafted to always score highest in relevance ranking
Excessive context assembly including irrelevant but sensitive document chunks in the LLM prompt
Re-ranking bias causing systematic suppression of certain document types or sources
Access control bypass in metadata filtering allowing cross-tenant document retrieval

nodes.label: LLM / Foundation Model
nodes.description: Large language model that processes the assembled context (retrieved documents) combined with the user query and system prompt to generate a natural language response. This is the core generative component. May be cloud-hosted (GPT-4, Claude, Gemini) or self-hosted (Llama, Mistral). Processes sensitive context including contracted rates, customer data, and compliance information.
vulnerabilities: Prompt injection via retrieved context causing the model to ignore system instructions and follow attacker commands
Sensitive information disclosure when the model reveals confidential data from its context window in responses
Hallucination generating fabricated rates, procedures, or compliance requirements that appear authoritative
System prompt leakage when crafted queries cause the model to reveal its internal instructions
Training data extraction through carefully constructed prompts that elicit memorized pre-training content
Unbounded token consumption from adversarial queries designed to maximize output length and API costs

nodes.label: System Prompt
nodes.description: Defines the LLM's role, behavioral constraints, output format requirements, topic boundaries, and safety guardrails. Contains instructions like domain restrictions, response length limits, citation requirements, and prohibited actions. This is a critical security component — its integrity determines the chatbot's behavioral envelope.
vulnerabilities: Prompt leakage through adversarial queries that trick the LLM into revealing system prompt contents
Instruction override when injected content in retrieved documents supersedes system prompt constraints
Insufficient guardrails allowing the model to operate outside intended domain boundaries
Hardcoded secrets if API keys or internal URLs are embedded in the system prompt text

nodes.label: Output Filter
nodes.description: Post-processing layer that validates LLM responses before delivery to the user. Performs PII detection and redaction, hallucination scoring against retrieved sources, response format validation, toxicity filtering, and ensures responses stay within authorized topic boundaries. Last line of defense before user-facing output.
vulnerabilities: PII detection gaps missing novel formats of personal data (e.g., coded customer references, internal rate IDs)
Hallucination checker false negatives when fabricated content is plausible but ungrounded
Filter bypass through encoding or formatting tricks that evade pattern-based detection
Insufficient validation allowing the model to output executable code or markdown injection

nodes.label: Response Delivery
nodes.description: Formats and transmits the validated response back to the user through the appropriate channel (chat widget, API response, Teams message, email). Handles response streaming, citation formatting, source attribution, and conversation state persistence.
vulnerabilities: Response tampering if delivery channel lacks integrity protection (e.g., HTTP without TLS)
Citation spoofing displaying fake source attributions that lend false credibility to hallucinated content
Conversation state manipulation if session data is stored client-side without server validation
Information leakage through verbose error messages exposing internal system details

nodes.label: Logging & Monitoring
nodes.description: Captures conversation logs, query-response pairs, usage metrics, retrieval performance data, error rates, and anomaly detection signals. Provides audit trail for compliance, enables abuse detection, tracks model performance degradation, and supports cost monitoring for API-based LLM usage.
vulnerabilities: Log injection attacks embedding malicious payloads in queries that execute when logs are viewed in dashboards
Sensitive data in logs if PII or confidential rates are stored in conversation records without redaction
Insufficient retention policies violating data protection regulations (GDPR, SOX) for logged conversations
Alert fatigue from poorly tuned anomaly detection missing genuine attack patterns in noise

nodes.label: Admin Panel
nodes.description: Management interface for knowledge base administration, system prompt engineering, analytics dashboards, user management, and model configuration. High-privilege access point that controls chatbot behavior, document ingestion, and safety guardrails. Typically accessed by product owners, ML engineers, and content managers.
vulnerabilities: Compromised admin credentials granting full control over system prompt, knowledge base, and safety guardrails
Privilege escalation allowing lower-privileged users to modify system prompts or disable output filters
Insecure document upload pipeline enabling injection of poisoned documents into the knowledge base
Audit trail gaps failing to log who modified system prompts or safety configurations and when

edges.label: Raw document chunks for context assembly
Blocked requests and rate limit events
Token usage, latency, and model metadata
PII detections, hallucination flags, filter actions
Analytics queries and audit log access
